High_voltage_performance_CEI: The BCM electrolyte shows an extremely high oxidative stability limit up to 3.5 V versus Mg on SS electrode in coin cells, with improved Coulombic efficiency and higher current density in subsequent cycles.

SEI: The SEI layer formed by the Mg[B(HFIP)4]2/DME-MgI2 electrolyte consists of MgF2 and MgI2 species, which facilitate fast and stable Mg plating/stripping, suppress side reactions, and reduce interface impedance.

CEI: The in-situ generated robust cathode electrolyte interphase (CEI) on cycled Mo6S8 is composed of hybrid components, including BmOn and CxNy, which facilitate Mg2+ desolvation and improve intercalation kinetics and cycling stability.
High_voltage_performance_CEI: The Mo6S8||Mg full cells with TMPLB electrolyte show a discharge capacity of 88.2 mAh g⁻¹ after 3000 cycles at 5 C, with superior capacity retention (94.3%). The oxidation stability on SS is approximately 3.0 V vs. Mg2+/Mg, but higher charge cut-off voltages like 2.1 V lead to uneven CEI and worse performance.
SEI: The solid electrolyte interphase (SEI) formed on Mg anode is conducive to Mg2+ transfer kinetics and cycling stability, composed of inorganic components like MgF2 and MgCl2 that allow Mg2+ conduction and suppress parasitic reactions, along with organic components.

The introduction of TPFPB induces the formation of a stable electronically insulating MgF2-rich SEI layer, which effectively prevents the decomposition of electrolytes and elevates the cycle life of batteries. It also suppresses side reactions and reduces interface impedance.
